{{Short description|Force with path-independent work}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Conservative force
| image = Conservative Force Gravity Example.svg
| caption = Work done by [[gravity]] on an object depends only on the change in height, not the path taken.
| topics = [[Force]], [[Potential energy]], [[Work (physics)]], [[Conservation of energy]]
}}

In [[physics]], a '''conservative force''' is a [[force]] with the property that the total [[Work (physics)|work]] done in moving a particle between two points is independent of the path taken. Equivalently, if a particle travels in a closed loop, the total work done by a conservative force is zero:

:<math>\oint \mathbf{F} \cdot d\mathbf{r} = 0</math>

Conservative forces derive from a [[scalar potential]] energy function <math>U(\mathbf{r})</math>:
:<math>\mathbf{F} = -\nabla U</math>

== Equivalent conditions ==
A force <math>\mathbf{F}</math> is conservative if and only if any of the following equivalent conditions holds:
# <math>\nabla \times \mathbf{F} = 0</math> (the curl is zero everywhere)
# <math>\mathbf{F} = -\nabla U</math> for some scalar potential <math>U</math>
# The work around any closed path is zero: <math>\oint\mathbf{F}\cdot d\mathbf{r} = 0</math>

== Examples ==
'''Conservative forces:'''
* [[Gravity|Gravitational force]]: <math>U = -G\frac{m_1 m_2}{r}</math>
* [[Coulomb's law|Electrostatic force]]: <math>U = k_e\frac{q_1 q_2}{r}</math>
* [[Hooke's law|Ideal spring force]]: <math>U = \frac{1}{2}kx^2</math>

'''Non-conservative forces:'''
* [[Friction]] — depends on path length, dissipates energy as heat
* [[Drag (physics)|Air resistance]] — velocity-dependent
* [[Magnetic force on a moving charge]] — depends on velocity direction
* Tension in inelastic materials

== Conservation of energy ==
{{Main|Conservation of energy}}
For a particle subject only to conservative forces, the total [[mechanical energy]] is conserved:
:<math>E = T + U = \frac{1}{2}mv^2 + U(\mathbf{r}) = \text{constant}</math>

This is because the work done equals the negative change in potential energy:
:<math>W = \int \mathbf{F} \cdot d\mathbf{r} = -\Delta U = \Delta T</math>

== Mathematical properties ==
* Conservative vector fields are [[irrotational vector field|irrotational]]: <math>\nabla \times \mathbf{F} = 0</math>
* In [[simply connected space]]s, irrotational fields are conservative
* The [[gradient theorem]] relates line integrals to potential differences

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Conservation of energy]]
* [[Force]]
* [[Potential energy]]
* [[Work (physics)]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Force]]
[[Category:Classical mechanics]]
[[Category:Potential theory]]
